items.reason: DEC-001 through DEC-020 are present in `docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md`, each with id, question, options_considered, choice, consequences, and projects_to. The new platform-shape decisions (DEC-011 through DEC-020) cover dictionary direction, canonical reconciliation, global enums, closed privacy classes, and migration generation as required by `docs/decisions/decisions-needed.md`.

`docs/decisions/decisions-pending.md` lists PEND-001 (target 2026-05-15, owner Andy, blocker 'Product priority on next runtime slice') and PEND-005 (target 2026-05-03, owner Andy, blocker 'CI capacity'). Both have owner, target_date, blocker, and rationale. Both target dates are in the future relative to the rubric's 2026-04-26 timestamp.

Each decision's consequences names a concrete artifact it removes. DEC-001: 'Removes per-function subject-to-person merge branches from `supabase/functions/lti-launch-handler/index.ts`'. DEC-007: 'Removes duplicate source-ID columns from every public table; `public.source_identifiers` owns ... instead of each table growing its own SIS/LTI/email columns'. DEC-010: 'Removes duplicated tenant `where tenant_id = ...` guard code from PostgREST callers'. Concrete removals throughout.

Each decision has three credible options bracketing the design space. DEC-001 considers per-spec tables (permissive), one collapsed person (aggressive), and the chosen middle. DEC-005 considers AGS-only canonical (aggressive collapse other specs into AGS), peer source-native stores (permissive), and the chosen separation. DEC-016 considers shared-only generated on demand (aggressive), per-spec canonical (permissive), and the chosen committed projection. These are real architectural choices.

`scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py --check` enforces that `dictionary/*.v1.json` are projections from `data/data-dictionary.seed.json`; it is wired into VERIFY.md and `scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py`. The seed's projection_summary reports 58 objects, 568 fields, and 565 controlled values across all five specs, and `spec_object`/`spec_field` blocks embed every projected element. Drift causes a build error.

Each spec field's canonical_field_id is namespaced to its own spec, e.g. OneRoster person.email is `canonical.oneroster-core.person.email`, not a shared canonical. The integration-governance dictionary's `lti_membership.email` and Caliper's `caliper_actor.identifier_value` have their own per-spec canonical IDs; they do not point to one shared canonical email field. DEC-017 declares that overlapping fields 'carry canonical_field_id pointing at the shared dictionary', but the seed's canonical_field_id values are spec-local identifiers, so the cross-spec overlap is unresolved at the data level.
items.traced_cause: data/data-dictionary.seed.json: introduce a shared canonical namespace (e.g. `canonical.platform.identity.email`) and rewrite every overlapping per-spec field's `canonical_field_id` to point at it, so OneRoster `person.email`, Caliper `caliper_actor.identifier_value`, LTI `lti_membership.email`, and any QTI candidate.email all reference the same canonical field. Add a checker that fails when overlapping spec fields carry distinct canonical IDs.

items.reason: Identity-shaped objects in each spec carry their own per-spec canonical_object_id (OneRoster person -> `canonical.oneroster-core.person`). The seed contains no shared canonical identity entity that Caliper `caliper_actor`, LTI `lti_membership`, and QTI candidate all point to. There is no `canonical.platform.identity` (or equivalent) object in the seed's `objects[]` with its own fields, allowed values, and projections. Unification exists only in DEC-001 prose.
items.traced_cause: data/data-dictionary.seed.json: add a shared canonical identity entity (e.g. `canonical.platform.identity` with fields like display_name, email, primary_role) as a first-class object in objects[], and set `canonical_object_id` on OneRoster person, Caliper caliper_actor, LTI lti_membership, and QTI candidate to that shared id. Update generate_spec_dictionaries.py and check_dictionary_artifacts.py to enforce a single shared identity object.

items.reason: Per-spec dictionaries declare independent enum lists. OneRoster person.primary_role inlines `student`/`teacher`/`administrator`/`guardian` with sourceStandard `OneRoster`. The integration-governance dictionary declares LTI message_type, lis_role, scope vocabularies separately, and Caliper declares actor_type/lis_role/profile values separately. There is no shared `role_family` (or similar) enum table with bidirectional crosswalks mapping OneRoster `student` <-> LTI `Learner` <-> Caliper actor types. DEC-018 mandates this but the seed implements only `shared_allowed_values` for status-style helpers, not a cross-spec role/role-family table.
items.traced_cause: data/data-dictionary.seed.json: add a top-level `shared_enums` table keyed by `shared_enum_id` (e.g. role_family, message_type) listing canonical values plus crosswalks to every spec-native value with sourceStandard. Update each enum field across per-spec dictionaries to set `shared_enum_id` instead of inline `allowed_values`, and add a checker that fails when an enum field has no `shared_enum_id` or when a spec-native value is missing from the crosswalk.

items.reason: DEC-019 declares the closed privacy class set as public, operational, directory, education_record, behavioral, sensitive, credential, privacy_governance, system, and forbids `depends_on_entity` and `depends_on_contents`. But `dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json` ships `caliper_entity.name` with `privacy_class: "depends_on_entity"` and `caliper_entity.extensions` with `privacy_class: "depends_on_contents"`. These escape-hatch values violate the closed enumeration the decision mandates.
items.traced_cause: dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json (and its seed projection in data/data-dictionary.seed.json): replace `depends_on_entity` and `depends_on_contents` with concrete classes from the closed list (likely `behavioral` or `education_record` per occurrence), and add a check_dictionary_artifacts assertion that every privacy_class value is drawn from the seed's `privacy_classes` list.

items.reason: The seed dictionary's objects do not declare a `relationships` array (or equivalent FK/cardinality/ownership graph). Foreign keys live only in `supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql` (e.g. `references public.classes(id)`, `references public.people(id)`). There is no generator that produces those migrations from the dictionary; DEC-020 declares the intent but no implementation exists. Migrations are hand-edited.
items.traced_cause: data/data-dictionary.seed.json: add a `relationships` array on each object declaring source_field, target_object, target_field, cardinality, ownership, and on_delete. Add scripts/generate_supabase_migrations.py that emits supabase/migrations/*.sql from that graph, and wire a --check mode into VERIFY.md so committed migrations must match the generator output.

items.reason: Every per-spec field carries a `decision_id` (e.g. OneRoster person.email -> `DEC-001-person-agent-subject`, person.primary_role -> `DEC-003-role-vocabulary`). `scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py` builds a decision_trace map from `docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md` and fails when a field cites an unknown decision_id or when a decision's produces_fields lists a missing field. The decision-trace JSON block in the register matches dictionary fields.

Five generators produce object/field/enum reference pages from the per-spec dictionaries (scripts/generate_oneroster_core.py, generate_qti_core.py, generate_case_core.py, generate_caliper_core.py, generate_integration_governance_core.py), each emitting Markdown and HTML, and check_dictionary_artifacts.py verifies object/field/value coverage. However, hand-written prose pages such as docs/lead-spec-accounting.md and docs/dictionary-coverage-matrix.md make load-bearing field claims (e.g. demographics field lists, score-scale fields) without linking to dictionary anchors, so prose claims aren't all backed by dictionary links.
items.traced_cause: docs/lead-spec-accounting.md and docs/dictionary-coverage-matrix.md: replace inline field/enum names with anchor links to the corresponding generated dictionary pages (e.g. site/docs/oneroster-core-dictionary.html#person-email), and add a doc-link checker that fails when a prose page mentions a field without linking to its dictionary anchor.

items.reason: The decision register renders as HTML at site/docs/decisions-standards-overlap-decisions.html and is linked from the portal. However, plain-language doc pages do not consistently explain WHY each concept exists. docs/lead-spec-accounting.md adds a 'Decision Citation Map' table with terse one-liners ('Tenant-owned roster and gradebook records are isolated by RLS'), but for many concepts (separate agent vs person, enrollment vs membership, CASE as canonical) there is no narrative doc page that walks a developer through the choice in plain language - the explanation is only in the 900-line decision register file. The substance_check explicitly says linking to a 900-line raw register as the only explanation fails.
items.traced_cause: docs/: add concept-level explainer pages (e.g. docs/concepts/identity.md, docs/concepts/enrollment-vs-membership.md, docs/concepts/standards-alignment.md) that explain the choice and the simplification it produced in plain language and link to the specific decision anchor. The buildability guide should reference these.

items.reason: There is no `docs/build-an-edtech-app.md` (or equivalent) in the evidence. README.md, PLAN.md, and site/index.html do not link to such a guide. The portal's 'First Integration Path' section in site/index.html (`pathList`) is populated dynamically from app.js but is not an end-to-end executable walkthrough. No file walks a layperson through (a) creating a school, (b) enrolling students, (c) posting a grade, (d) linking to a CASE standard URI, (e) reading a Caliper feed with copy-pasteable curl/SQL.
items.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md: create the end-to-end guide with copy-pasteable curl/SQL for each of the five required steps against the live Supabase runtime (REST + Edge Functions), with every field and enum value linked to its dictionary anchor. Link the guide from site/index.html and README.md.

items.reason: The portal navigation links to dictionary pages, decision register, OpenAPI pages, coverage matrix, and lead-spec accounting. But there is no link checker output in evidence, and the missing buildability guide means the apex doc page is not reachable. The coverage matrix references docs/dictionaries/README.md and docs/dictionaries/*.md (layperson dictionaries) which exist according to README, but their HTML rendering and reachability cannot be verified. Generated dictionary pages have a known duplicate/orphan problem flagged in spec-gap-backlog item 25.
items.traced_cause: scripts/build_site_docs.py and VERIFY.md: add a link checker (e.g. linkchecker or a Python script) that fails on 404s and on dictionary entries / decisions / endpoints not reachable from site/index.html within three clicks. Resolve generated dictionary portal canonical URL cleanup (backlog item 25) so each generated page has one canonical link.

items.reason: Reading only `docs/`, a layperson cannot produce executable artifacts for the five required steps. (1) Create school: docs/ has no instruction to insert into `public.organizations` and no guide on tenant_id sourcing. (2) Create class with enrollments: no walkthrough of organizations -> people -> classes -> enrollments -> source_identifiers FKs. (3) Post grade: docs/supabase-hosted-database.md shows an Edge Function curl but does not explain how to obtain SUPABASE_USER_JWT with a tenant claim or how to choose scoreStatus enum values. (4) Link assignment to CASE URI: CASE has only generated dictionary pages, no runtime, no documented import path, and no field on line_items pointing at CASE. (5) Read Caliper feed: only an ingestion receipt path exists; there is no documented read endpoint or query for a class activity feed. No buildability guide exists to bridge these gaps.
items.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md plus the underlying dictionary/decision gaps: (a) write the end-to-end guide; (b) DEC-006/CASE: add a documented assignment->CASE alignment field on line_items in the seed and a runtime path or static example; (c) Caliper: document a read query (REST or function) for class events; (d) auth: docs/ must explain how to mint a tenant-scoped user JWT for the non-test reader.

items.reason: scripts/evaluate_platform.py emits a `traced_cause` field on every non-pass item (DEFAULT_TRACED_CAUSES map plus normalize_traced_cause helper) and a `buildability_gaps` array with traced causes. The current report at site/api/spec-conformance.json is the advisory deterministic report; the evaluator output (this run) populates traced_cause for every non-pass item by design.

VERIFY.md ends with `python3 scripts/evaluate_platform.py --output site/api/platform-evaluation.json`, and scripts/codex_loop.py calls run_evaluator both before and after each iteration. evaluate_platform.py writes per-item status, traced_cause for non-pass items, and a buildability_gaps array to site/api/platform-evaluation.json.

scripts/codex_loop.py defines read_overrides(), overrides_paused() (checks for the literal PAUSE token on its own line, ignoring HTML-commented examples), and format_overrides_for_prompt(). The PAUSE check runs at the top of each iteration BEFORE Codex is invoked ('if overrides_paused(overrides): print(...); return 0'). Overrides are injected into build_prompt under a 'Human overrides from `docs/loop-overrides.md` (AUTHORITATIVE ...)' section.

scripts/codex_loop.py terminates on the evaluator's `done` flag both before and after each iteration: 'if before_eval and before_eval.get("done"): print("Evaluator reports `done` before any work..."); return 0' and 'if after_eval and after_eval.get("done"): print("Evaluator reports `done` after this iteration. Stopping the loop."); return 0'. Hard caps (max_iterations, stall_limit) are safety rails, not the primary gate.

buildability_gaps.gap: No documented HTTP/SQL pattern for creating an organization row in the live runtime; docs/ does not show how to obtain a tenant-scoped JWT, what tenant_id to use, or whether there is an Edge Function for org creation versus direct PostgREST insert.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md: add Step 1 with curl against Supabase REST or a new org-admin Edge Function, including how to mint a tenant-scoped JWT and which dictionary fields on `public.organizations` are required.

buildability_gaps.gap: Layperson cannot derive the FK chain (organizations -> academic_sessions -> courses -> classes -> people -> enrollments -> source_identifiers) from docs/ alone; docs do not surface relationships because the dictionary has no relational graph (DEC-020 not implemented in data).
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: data/data-dictionary.seed.json: add `relationships` to objects per DEC-020. docs/build-an-edtech-app.md: add Step 2 walking through each insert with the FK fields linked to dictionary anchors.

buildability_gaps.gap: Edge Function curl in docs/supabase-hosted-database.md uses $SUPABASE_USER_JWT with no instructions on how to obtain it, and scoreStatus enum values are not linked to a global enum (only OneRoster-native list).
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: data/data-dictionary.seed.json: add a shared_enum for score_status per DEC-018. docs/build-an-edtech-app.md: add Step 3 with a documented JWT-minting flow (Supabase Auth signup or admin issuance) and link scoreStatus values to the shared enum.

buildability_gaps.gap: There is no runtime CASE store, no field on line_items linking to a CASE item URI, and no documented alignment endpoint. CASE is documented as 'Docs only' in docs/spec-gap-backlog.md.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: data/data-dictionary.seed.json: add a `line_item.alignment_uri` field (or analogous join) per DEC-006. supabase/migrations: add a CASE seed table or alignment column. docs/build-an-edtech-app.md: add Step 4 showing how to write the CASE URI and read it back.

buildability_gaps.gap: Only the ingestion receipt path is documented (POST /caliper-event-ingestion). docs/ does not document a read endpoint or query for a class activity feed; immutable raw-event store is explicitly deferred.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: supabase/functions: add a caliper-event-feed reader (or a documented PostgREST view) returning class events under tenant RLS. docs/build-an-edtech-app.md: add Step 5 with a curl against that reader and link event_type / action values to the shared Caliper enum.

buildability_gaps.gap: Cross-spec field unification is not in the data: OneRoster person.email, Caliper actor identifier_value, and LTI lti_membership.email each have spec-local canonical_field_ids, so a developer who joins these systems must still implement spec-to-spec conversion.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: data/data-dictionary.seed.json: introduce a shared canonical namespace and rewrite overlapping fields' canonical_field_id to the same shared id (DEC-017 enforcement).

projections.note: Structural per-decision projection index generated from `projects_to`; semantic contradictions are graded by the projections_match_reality rubric item.

decisions.title: DEC-001 Person, User, Actor, Profile, and Subject
decisions.choice: The platform has a canonical `person` for known school people and a broader `agent` concept for non-person actors. Credential subjects and Caliper actors resolve to `person` only when policy and identifiers allow it; otherwise they remain linked external agents.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-002 Class, Course, Context, Group, Course Section, and Organization
decisions.choice: The platform separates `course` from `class`. `course` is the catalog/template. `class` is the scheduled teaching section. LTI contexts and Caliper groups can map to a class, course, or other learning context. Common Cartridge organizations are content outlines, not school organizations or scheduled classes.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-003 Roles
decisions.choice: Store source roles exactly, then map them to platform role families: `learner`, `educator`, `administrator`, `guardian`, `mentor`, `staff`, `tool`, `issuer`, and `unknown`.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-004 Enrollment and Membership
decisions.choice: `enrollment` is the canonical roster participation record. `membership` is a contextual service/event view that may be derived from enrollment or captured from an external system.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-005 Results, Scores, Outcomes, and Grade Events
decisions.choice: The platform uses `line_item` as the gradebook target and `result` as the learner's current gradebook state. LTI AGS Score is an incoming write/update message. LTI AGS Result and OneRoster Result map to current result state. QTI outcomes are assessment-runtime variables projected into results only when a delivery or scoring workflow declares that mapping. Caliper GradeEvent is event history, not the gradebook source of truth.

decisions.title: DEC-006 Standards Alignment
decisions.choice: CASE is the canonical standards graph. Other standards store alignment as source metadata and resolve known targets to CASE item identifiers or URIs.

decisions.title: DEC-007 Identifiers
decisions.choice: Internal records have platform IDs. Standard-shaped endpoints expose the standard-native ID as the primary contract when required. Every external ID is stored in a source identifier crosswalk with source system, identifier type, and object relationship.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-008 Time, School Sessions, and Event Timestamps
decisions.choice: Separate calendar periods, event timestamps, availability windows, and validity periods. Store timestamps in UTC with source timezone/offset when provided. Store OneRoster AcademicSession as the school calendar backbone.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-009 Content, Resources, Packages, and Launch Links
decisions.choice: The platform uses a broad `resource` concept for discoverable learning objects, with subtype-specific records for cartridge resources, QTI packages/items/tests, LTI resource links, and Caliper event entities.

decisions.title: DEC-010 Tenant-Owned Data and Shared Reference Data
decisions.choice: Tenant-owned operational records carry tenant boundaries and row-level policy. Public CASE frameworks, public standards metadata, public certification fixtures, and optionally public item banks live in shared reference namespaces. Tenants adopt, pin, override, or privately extend shared reference data through explicit records.

projections.detail: Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-011 Privacy Surfaces
decisions.choice: Generated docs may describe every field and privacy class because they are schema documentation, not records. Live PostgREST may expose tenant-owned `public`, `operational`, `directory`, and `education_record` records only through tenant RLS and scope policy. Edge Functions may expose `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, or sensitive operational fields only through caller JWT, purpose, scope, tenant RLS, and audit logging when the read is sensitive. Static `site/api/*.json` mirrors may contain only synthetic demo records from the seeded `.test` tenant and may include `public`, `operational`, and `directory` fields such as demo email; they may not contain real tenant data, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive_pii`, or secret/system values.

decisions.title: DEC-012 Runtime Coverage Per Spec
decisions.choice: OneRoster core is runtime-backed now through local SQLite, hosted Supabase PostgREST, RLS, and live tests. QTI and CASE are doc-only/generated dictionary projections until runtime slices are built. Caliper, LTI, Security Framework, and Data Privacy have partial runtime backing through authenticated Edge Function receipt/governance paths, but not full standard conformance flows. Full runtime for QTI import, CASE search, Caliper raw-event projection, LTI Advantage services, production OAuth, and privacy workflows remains future work with target dates in the decision/pending work queue.

decisions.title: DEC-013 Audit Response Truth
decisions.choice: Audited Edge Functions must use truthful responses when they include an `audit` block. If the function cannot read the written `audit_log` rows back under the caller's JWT and request identifier, it must omit the audit block or return an explicit error; it must not hard-code `logged` counts or field lists.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-014 Static Mirror Policy
decisions.choice: Static mirrors are allowed as documentation fixtures for GitHub Pages and offline inspection. They are not the canonical runtime contract; live Supabase REST and Edge Functions are the runtime contract. Mirrors must be generated from the same synthetic demo seed as the local demo, must carry source metadata, and may only include privacy classes allowed by `DEC-011-privacy-surfaces`.

decisions.title: DEC-015 Service-Role Policy
decisions.choice: Request-scoped runtime code and Edge Functions must pass the caller's `Authorization` bearer token to Supabase so RLS applies. Service-role access is allowed only for allowlisted admin or test fixture operations in `docs/admin-operations.md`, with a named operation ID, caller, why-user-JWT-insufficient reason, guardrails, and recent review date. Tests may use service role to create temporary Auth users, but not to read tenant data or satisfy the assertion under test.

decisions.title: DEC-016 Dictionary Generation Direction
decisions.choice: `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` is upstream. `dictionary/*.v1.json` files are generated projections; hand-editing a per-spec projection is a build error once the generator lands.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-017 Canonical Field Reconciliation
decisions.choice: Every overlapping spec field carries `canonical_field_id` pointing at the shared dictionary. Spec-native names remain for conformance; cross-spec app code uses the canonical field identity.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-018 Global Enum Crosswalks
decisions.choice: Allowed values are global dictionary objects. Each enum field references `shared_enum_id`; the shared enum maps every spec-native value to and from a canonical value.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-019 Closed Privacy Classes
decisions.choice: Closed keys are `public`, `operational`, `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive`, `credential`, `privacy_governance`, and `system`. `depends_on_entity` and `depends_on_contents` are invalid.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-020 Relational Graph Migrations
decisions.choice: Shared dictionary objects own source field, target object, target field, cardinality, ownership, and delete behavior. Supabase migrations are generated from that graph.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
